Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

I don't understand the logic of the patch.

If the reply is saved in the ted_reply_last, it means the reply has been released because it will not be used anymore to reconstruct reply in case of resend. It is saved only to record on disk the highest transno of this export.

The reply is released if and only if the same tag has been reused (tgt_handle_tag()) by a request or the last_xid of an incoming request indicates the client did receive replies for lower xid (tgt_handle_received_xid()). In both cases, it is not expected that the client resends the request with that xid.

Patch Set 1:

Thanks for this detailed scenario. It helps a lot.

At step 4, I don't understand why the client does resend the request again, although he already resent it at step 1.

At step 4, what is the value of the last_xid embedded in the request ? The semantic of the last_xid field is to contain the highest xid for which the client received a reply. Is this semantic not respected during recovery ?

Note that the way last_xid field is managed will change with patch http://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/16759/ .

Patch Set 1:

At step1, the client sent the request to replay, and at step4, the client resent the request for it didn't received
the reply of the request sent at step1 in time.

the XIDs are the same value for resent request.

Patch Set 1:

What are the values of the rq_last_xid field at the different steps ?

I don't see why the reply is released at step 5. The rcvd_xid parameter comes from the rq_last_xid value which should be strictly lower than the rq_xid value.

Patch Set 1:

Now that patch http://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/16759/ has been landed into master, the "last_xid" stored in the request cannot be larger than the "rq_xid" of the request.

Patch Set 1:

Okay, Thanks
then this issue could be marked as a duplicate of LU-5951

Abandoned

it's not needed after patch http://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/16759/ is landed

parents.subject: LU-7077 target: avoid using possible error return NULL pointer

commit.subject: LU-6841 target: check the last reply for resent
commit.message:
LU-6841 target: check the last reply for resent

if there is only one reply and its transno equals to the last
transno of the client, it will be saved to "ted_reply_last"
in tg_export_data instead of being linked to "ted_reply_list",
then it is needed to check "ted_reply_last" for resent request.

Change-Id: I8736501ab49f266ed19f346c2f15fad436772270
Signed-off-by: Hongchao Zhang <hongchao.zhang@intel.com>
